jokes.text: Pawnee is, as you all know, the fourth most obese city in America. Soon to be number three. We're coming for you, San Antonio. No, we are not. We are slimming down.
jokes.setup: Chris proudly announces Pawnee's obesity ranking like it's an achievement
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with 'No, we are not' when he realizes how it sounds
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Chris's enthusiasm turning to self-correction. Shows character's optimistic but sometimes misguided approach.

jokes.text: I will be running backwards up the big hill behind the Walmart.
jokes.setup: Chris suggests various health activities
jokes.punchline: His personal workout is comically specific and unnecessarily difficult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris Traeger moment - absurdly specific fitness routine that only he would think is normal.

jokes.text: Don't freak out, but Joe from Sewage just unhooked your bra with his eyes.
jokes.setup: Ann notices Joe checking out Leslie
jokes.punchline: Hilariously dramatic description of his leering
jokes.explanation: Vivid, absurd imagery that perfectly captures inappropriate workplace behavior.

jokes.text: Listen, if you're looking for a good time, why don't you come on down to the Toilet Party? That's what we call the Sewage Department.
jokes.setup: Joe tries to hit on Leslie
jokes.punchline: His department's nickname is hilariously inappropriate for seduction
jokes.explanation: Crude but fitting for the sleazy character. The department nickname adds absurdity.

jokes.text: Red meat can cause sluggishness, heart disease, even impotence. Has the opposite effect on me.
jokes.setup: Chris lists negative effects of red meat
jokes.punchline: Ron's deadpan claim that it affects him oppositely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson response - confident, absurd, and perfectly in character. Great contrast with Chris.

jokes.text: Is that a fried turkey leg inside a grilled hamburger? If so, yes. Delicious.
jokes.setup: Chris suggests a turkey burger
jokes.punchline: Ron's interpretation is comically excessive and meaty
jokes.explanation: Showcases Ron's carnivorous extremism through his warped understanding of 'healthy' alternatives.

jokes.text: Why would anyone do that to themselves?
jokes.setup: Chris explains what a turkey burger actually is
jokes.punchline: Ron treats healthy eating like self-harm
jokes.explanation: Ron's genuine confusion about healthy choices is both funny and character-consistent.

jokes.text: What do I get if I win? The rarest jewel of all. Victory over me, Ron Swanson.
jokes.setup: Chris asks what he gets if he wins the cook-off
jokes.punchline: Ron's overly dramatic description of beating him as a prize
jokes.explanation: Ron's ego presented as a treasure is perfectly in character and amusingly pompous.

jokes.text: I did get in trouble for shaking one... Yeah, I'm not allowed there anymore.
jokes.setup: Ben mentions getting in trouble at the snow globe museum
jokes.punchline: Leslie escalates - she's completely banned
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mild trouble to total banishment, fits Leslie's tendency toward extremes.

jokes.text: Uh...
jokes.setup: Leslie asks Ben out and he's clearly flustered
jokes.punchline: His inability to form words shows his awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective awkward beat that shows Ben's internal conflict about dating Leslie.

jokes.text: Bye, babe.
jokes.setup: After Ben's awkward rejection
jokes.punchline: Leslie's forced casual 'babe' makes it more awkward
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Leslie trying to play it cool when it's clearly not cool.

jokes.text: He was like, 'Huh? What? Uh... Bye.' And then, he walked into an office that wasn't his.
jokes.setup: Leslie describes Ben's reaction to being asked out
jokes.punchline: The detail about the wrong office shows how flustered he was
jokes.explanation: Good comedic detail that shows the extent of Ben's awkwardness through physical comedy.

jokes.text: I was... Crap on a crayfish. That really stings. I haven't pricked you, yet.
jokes.setup: Leslie reacts dramatically to what seems like the blood test
jokes.punchline: Ann reveals she hasn't even done it yet - Leslie is reacting to imagination
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Leslie's tendency to overreact, plus her unique exclamation.

jokes.text: You're a monster. Still haven't done it, yet.
jokes.setup: Leslie again reacts to the test
jokes.punchline: Ann repeats that she still hasn't done anything
jokes.explanation: Callback to the previous gag, reinforces Leslie's anticipatory anxiety.

jokes.text: Who the hell is Forp? I don't know. I couldn't really hear him. It sounded like his name was Forp.
jokes.setup: April mentions a guy she was talking to
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical name 'Forp' delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: 'Forp' is wonderfully absurd and memorable, delivered with April's characteristic indifference.

jokes.text: I'm getting healthier snacks for the shoeshine stand. Chris is a food genius. Did you know that the food you eat becomes energy? Yeah. Boom. That's spaghetti. Nachos. That's a cookie.
jokes.setup: Andy explains Chris taught him about nutrition
jokes.punchline: His examples show he completely missed the point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - earnest but completely missing the health concept.

jokes.text: That's my husband.
jokes.setup: Looking at Andy's terrible food choices
jokes.punchline: April's resigned acknowledgment of Andy's foolishness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - April's dry delivery shows her loving exasperation with Andy.

jokes.text: Yellow-haired female. Likes waffles and news. Sexy, well-read blonde. Loves the sweeter things in life.
jokes.setup: Leslie's first attempt at a dating profile
jokes.punchline: Ann's much better version shows how bad Leslie's was
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing Leslie's clinical approach vs. what actually sounds appealing.

jokes.text: Organizing my agenda. Wait, that doesn't sound fun. Jamming on my planner.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to make her boring hobby sound exciting
jokes.punchline: 'Jamming' doesn't make planning sound any cooler
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's inability to understand what makes things appealing to others.

jokes.text: Upstairs, there is this mural of wildflowers. And I like to sit on a bench in front of it. Really? It could be anywhere in the world. Paris, Hawaii, the Grand Canyon. Nope. Just the bench in front of the mural.
jokes.setup: Ann suggests romantic destinations
jokes.punchline: Leslie insists on the least romantic option possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - preferring the safe, controlled government building over actual adventure.

jokes.text: What about, like, an actual meadow where wildflowers are? Eww, Ann. I'm scared of bees. Mural.
jokes.setup: Ann suggests the real thing
jokes.punchline: Leslie's fear of bees makes her prefer fake nature
jokes.explanation: Reveals Leslie's contradictions - loves nature but from a safe distance. 'Mural' as final word is perfect.

jokes.text: They're condescending.
jokes.setup: Leslie has no opinion on turtles
jokes.punchline: Her specific reason is wonderfully random
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and specific - attributing human personality flaws to turtles is wonderfully unexpected.

jokes.text: He's dark and mysterious. And he can sing. And he plays the organ. I think you just described the Phantom of the Opera.
jokes.setup: Leslie describes her ideal man
jokes.punchline: Ann points out it's exactly the Phantom of the Opera
jokes.explanation: Good recognition of Leslie's inadvertently specific and problematic romantic ideals.

jokes.text: I buy my burger ingredients at Food and Stuff, a discount food outlet equidistant from my home and my work. I came here for the same reason people go to the zoo.
jokes.setup: Ron explains why he's at a health food store
jokes.punchline: He's treating healthy people like zoo animals
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron moment - his disdain for health culture expressed through perfect analogy.

jokes.text: Shh. Look at that thing. Nature is amazing.
jokes.setup: Ron observing something in the health food store
jokes.punchline: He's treating a health food customer like a wild animal
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that extends the zoo metaphor with Ron's fake wildlife documentary tone.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. A 98% match? That's a soul mate-level match. I've never seen anything this high before.
jokes.setup: Ann is excited about Leslie's dating match
jokes.punchline: Sets up the reveal of who the match is
jokes.explanation: Functional setup for the Tom reveal, but not funny on its own.

jokes.text: Craig, your service is crap. Can you be more specific? Yes. Your soul mate match was totally wrong for me. I mean, I like him as a friend and everything, but I'd never go out with him. He's like a little sister to me.
jokes.setup: Leslie complains about her dating match
jokes.punchline: She describes Tom as 'like a little sister' which is hilariously emasculating
jokes.explanation: Good emasculating description that shows Leslie's complete romantic disinterest in Tom.

jokes.text: Really? Well, that's not gonna last. Excuse me? You heard me. Your marriage is a sham. Goodbye, Craig.
jokes.setup: Craig mentions he met his wife on the site
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately attacks his marriage
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's tendency to go nuclear when frustrated, escalating to personal attacks.

jokes.text: He seems nice. Eh, he kind of lives in a barn.
jokes.setup: Ann asks about Andy
jokes.punchline: April's casual dismissal of a major red flag
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - treating homelessness as a minor quirk with complete nonchalance.

jokes.text: But for whatever reason, right now, only douchey guys are buying what you're selling. So, I should go and ask them what they think I'm selling. A douche-vestigation.
jokes.setup: Ann explains Leslie's dating problem
jokes.punchline: Leslie's portmanteau 'douche-vestigation'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Leslie's approach to solving problems through investigation.

jokes.text: She can't be in a wheelchair. No canes. No gray hair. So, basically, you're just attracted to me because I'm not an elderly person. Yeah. And as I aforementioned, you have a killer dumpster.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks Joe what he looks for in women
jokes.punchline: His standards are hilariously low and his compliment is crude
jokes.explanation: Shows Joe's crudeness while revealing the low bar Leslie apparently clears for some men.

jokes.text: Would you like to sample our vegan bacon? 100% meatless. Yes, please.
jokes.setup: Health store employee offers Ron vegan bacon
jokes.punchline: Ron agrees, setting up his reaction
jokes.explanation: Setup for Ron's reaction to fake meat, but not funny by itself.

jokes.text: Sir? Is there a problem? I'm just making sure no one ever has to eat this. I don't think I can give you any more. I want one.
jokes.setup: Ron is eating all the vegan bacon samples
jokes.punchline: He's doing it to protect others, while Andy actually wants to try it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron move - turning his disgust into a public service while Andy shows genuine curiosity.

jokes.text: Fresh lettuce is my all-time favorite food. What's your favorite food? Well, I take Skittles, and I put it between two Starbursts. You know what I call it? Skittle sandwich? That's pretty good. No, I call it Andy's mouth surprise.
jokes.setup: April claims healthy eating, Andy describes his creation
jokes.punchline: 'Andy's mouth surprise' is a terrible name for an already terrible food
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between April's fake health claims and Andy's sugar creation with a hilariously inappropriate name.

jokes.text: I call sandwiches sammies, sandoozles, or Adam Sandlers. Air conditioners are cool blasterz, with a Z. I don't know where that came from. I call cakes big ol' cookies. I call noodles long-ass rice. Fried chicken is fry-fry chicky-chick. Chicken parm is chicky-chicky parm-parm. Chicken cacciatore? Chicky catch. I call eggs pre-birds or future birds. Root beer is super water. Tortillas are bean blankies. And I call forks food rakes.
jokes.setup: Tom explains his food nicknames
jokes.punchline: The entire ridiculous list of unnecessary renamings
jokes.explanation: Legendary Tom moment - perfectly captures his need to rebrand everything. Each nickname gets progressively more absurd. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: First wish? I have a huge house with a ton of balconies. And I would just stand out there and survey my empire, like a drug dealer in a Michael Bay movie.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks Tom about three wishes
jokes.punchline: His first wish is hilariously specific and materialistic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom fantasy - shallow, specific, and referencing pop culture. The drug dealer comparison is unexpectedly dark.

jokes.text: Okay. I'm the CEO of the Spike TV network. And my best friend slash personal assistant is Oscar winner Jamie Foxx. And we create a raunchy animated series based on our friendship, called Tommy and the Foxx.
jokes.setup: Tom's second wish
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and narcissistic fantasy about befriending Jamie Foxx
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's entertainment industry delusions and his need to insert himself into celebrity friendships.

jokes.text: They remake Point Break. I play both roles. Keanu and Swayze.
jokes.setup: Tom's third wish
jokes.punchline: He wants to play both leads in a Point Break remake
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd final wish that captures Tom's ego and delusion. Playing both roles is wonderfully ridiculous.

jokes.text: I said one thing. Honey? Pinwheel.
jokes.setup: Andy asks for multiple items at the store
jokes.punchline: April's random pet names have no logic
jokes.explanation: Shows April's weird way of showing affection through random, disconnected pet names.

jokes.text: I once kissed a girl in college. Eight. Where I graduated summa cum laude in history. One. Zero. Negative a billion. Don't talk about it anymore, please.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to interest Tom in her accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Tom's interest spikes for the lesbian story then crashes for academics
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing Tom's shallow interests - titillated by lesbian experimentation but bored by intelligence.

jokes.text: I love Food and Stuff. It's where I buy all of my food. And most of my stuff.
jokes.setup: Ron in confessional about his shopping
jokes.punchline: The literal interpretation of the store name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan - taking the absurd store name completely literally with no irony.

jokes.text: Nope. Just the crows and the beef.
jokes.setup: Cashier asks if Ron needs anything else
jokes.punchline: Ron's shopping list is hilariously minimal and weird
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd shopping list that raises so many questions. 'Crows and beef' is memorably weird.

jokes.text: 98% match. Soul mate-level match. You wanna date me! This is a date!
jokes.setup: Leslie reveals the dating site match
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately assumes Leslie orchestrated a date
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's ego and his tendency to misread situations in his favor.

jokes.text: Leslie Knope, Tom Haverford / Dating in the day, dating in the night / Dating all day 'cause he's keeping it tight / All right, Tom. Enough. / Dating in the car, dating on the floor / Dating everywhere 'cause she wants some more
jokes.setup: Tom thinks he and Leslie are dating
jokes.punchline: His terrible improvised rap about their 'relationship'
jokes.explanation: Cringey but character-appropriate - shows Tom's delusional confidence and terrible artistic skills.

jokes.text: Oh, this is perfect for us. Three-bedroom, and... Oh, God, Les. It has that dream closet you've always wanted. A walk-in closet.
jokes.setup: Tom is looking at real estate
jokes.punchline: He's planning their life together based on one fake date
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tom's delusion - he's already planning their domestic life together.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, boo. What's wrong? Where did you go? Come back to me. I need you, boo.
jokes.setup: Leslie is clearly uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Tom's overly intimate response to her discomfort
jokes.explanation: Cringey escalation that shows Tom's complete misreading of Leslie's emotional state.

jokes.text: There is some weird juju in this room right now.
jokes.setup: Ben notices the awkward tension between Leslie and Tom
jokes.punchline: His awkward way of acknowledging the uncomfortable situation
jokes.explanation: Ben's awkward acknowledgment of the obvious tension, fitting his uncomfortable position.

jokes.text: You know what else gets people's heart rate up? Doing it. Talking about sex with my boss.
jokes.setup: Discussion about getting heart rates up
jokes.punchline: Tom's inappropriate sexual comment in front of Ben
jokes.explanation: Creates cringe but feels a bit forced - Tom crossing professional boundaries inappropriately.

jokes.text: I can't fight this feeling anymore. You and I, we're dating. You should be so lucky.
jokes.setup: Tom continues his delusion
jokes.punchline: Leslie's cutting response about how 'lucky' he'd be
jokes.explanation: Leslie's sharp putdown shows her exasperation while maintaining her dignity.

jokes.text: They should fix that.
jokes.setup: Tom presumably walked into something
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact response to his own clumsiness
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with Tom's typical refusal to accept responsibility.

jokes.text: Look. Cucumber flower. Wow, that's so cute. Wait, that's a garnish. You're not supposed to eat that. What?
jokes.setup: Andy finds something pretty on his plate
jokes.punchline: He's eating decorative garnish without realizing it
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Andy - finding beauty in something while completely missing its actual purpose.

jokes.text: Girl likes Indian food. What can I say?
jokes.setup: Tom explains the situation to Ben
jokes.punchline: His reductive explanation that avoids the real story
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's ego - he presents the story to make himself look good while being technically truthful.

jokes.text: And as much as it pains me to admit this, it was not disgusting. I'm just saying. She knows what to do. It was stirring. It felt like...
jokes.setup: Tom describes kissing Leslie
jokes.punchline: His reluctant admission that she's a good kisser
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's surprise at being attracted to Leslie despite their incompatibility.

jokes.text: This tastes as delicious as Beyoncé smells. I'm guessing.
jokes.setup: Chris tastes the turkey burger
jokes.punchline: His weirdly specific comparison to Beyoncé's smell
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird Chris moment - overly specific celebrity reference with the honest 'I'm guessing' qualifier.

jokes.text: Stop being so pretentious, Kyle. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Kyle mentions saffron and umami
jokes.punchline: Andy calls out pretentious food talk then immediately apologizes
jokes.explanation: Great Andy moment - accidentally smart observation followed by immediate politeness.

jokes.text: Here's mine. It's a hamburger, made out of meat, on a bun, with nothing. Add ketchup if you want. I couldn't care less.
jokes.setup: After Chris's elaborate turkey burger description
jokes.punchline: Ron's aggressively simple burger description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - deliberately anti-fancy presentation that wins through honesty and simplicity. Great contrast with Chris's overwrought description.

jokes.text: Granted, it's been a long time since I've had a hamburger.
jokes.setup: Chris is confused why his burger lost
jokes.punchline: He admits he hasn't eaten his competition in years
jokes.explanation: Reveals Chris's fundamental flaw - trying to improve something he doesn't understand anymore.

jokes.text: Hard work never pays off. Cooking is dumb. I swear on this dead crow that I will never cook for you.
jokes.setup: Andy is disappointed about the burger contest
jokes.punchline: His dramatic vow sworn on a dead crow
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Ron's shopping while showing Andy's dramatic overreaction to disappointment.

jokes.text: He oversees every department. It simply can't happen.
jokes.setup: Chris explains his no-dating policy
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly describing exactly why Ben can't date Leslie
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony - Chris is explaining exactly why Ben and Leslie can't date, unknowing that's who Ben was asking about.

jokes.text: Hey. Well, I think I'm allergic to chutney. Also, what's chutney? No clue.
jokes.setup: Leslie approaches Ben after the burger contest
jokes.punchline: She's allergic to something she can't even identify
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - being allergic to something you can't define is wonderfully Leslie.

jokes.text: It's like door number two on Let's Make a Deal. Do you want the thing that you have, that you know you like, but isn't perfect, or do you give it up for what's behind door number two?
jokes.setup: Leslie reflects on online dating
jokes.punchline: Her game show analogy for dating decisions
jokes.explanation: Smart analogy that shows Leslie's analytical approach to romance, though it reveals she's settling.

jokes.text: I think I like what I have. I'm gonna try to make it work with Tom. I'm kidding!
jokes.setup: Leslie seems to choose the status quo
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was joking about dating Tom
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that relieves tension about Leslie actually considering Tom romantically.

jokes.text: I made 26 profiles, each designed to attract a different type of girl. Tom A. Haverford. Sporty and sexy. Tom B. Haverford, smooth and soulful. Which letter did you get? N, Tom N. Haverford. The N stands for nerd!
jokes.setup: Tom explains his multiple dating profiles
jokes.punchline: Leslie matched with his nerd profile that he never checks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom scheme that backfires - his fake nerd persona is what attracted Leslie, showing their incompatibility.

jokes.text: Tom N. Haverford collects globes. Great. That's enough. His favorite movie is books.
jokes.setup: Tom explains his nerd profile details
jokes.punchline: His 'favorite movie is books' shows he doesn't understand nerds
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd line that shows Tom's complete misunderstanding of intellectual interests. 'Favorite movie is books' is wonderfully nonsensical.

jokes.text: Every time I want you to shut up, from now on.
jokes.setup: Tom continues rambling about his profiles
jokes.punchline: Donna's threat about future conversations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna - direct, threatening, and establishing a pattern for dealing with Tom's nonsense.
